workflow_name: Internal Account Review Transcript -> Team Actions
user_message: Can you extract the partner engagement from this exec meeting notes for Treyarch Global? I met with them last week but can't remember the details.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: partner engagement Discussion - Treyarch Global
Date: 2024-02-08
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Laura Schmidt (Treyarch Global), Raj Mehta (Microsoft), Michelle (Treyarch Global)

[00:02:00] Laura Schmidt (Treyarch Global): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on partner engagement from our last session.

[00:08:00] Laura Schmidt (Treyarch Global): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about technical validation. Can we address those?

[00:11:00] Laura Schmidt (Treyarch Global): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about timeline constraints. Can we address those?

[00:16:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on cost optimization and found that there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us.

[00:21:00] Michelle (Treyarch Global): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about integration complexity. Can we address those?

[00:26:00] Laura Schmidt (Treyarch Global): On the partner engagement front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around cost optimization.

[00:28:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): The timeline for security compliance is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:32:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about timeline constraints. Can we address those?

[00:34:00] Laura Schmidt (Treyarch Global): Let me add some context -- we've been working on budget approval and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:37:00] Michelle (Treyarch Global): On the partner engagement front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around data residency.

handoffs.condition: team coaching or risk review input

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF52-P trap (Coaching-notes output isn't first-class artifact in current skill set); produces complete Team action items with owners, coaching notes (constructive frame), customer-facing draft (sanitized
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF52-P failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF52-P failure mode (Coaching-notes output isn't first-class artifact in current skill set)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct partner engagement decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF52-P -- Coaching-notes output isn't first-class artifact in current skill set
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
